title: Beginner Project
summary: Build a complete grade calculator that uses everything learned so far

sections.title: Putting It All Together
sections.content: This project combines variables, arrays, loops, functions, control flow, and objects into one program. Building a complete tool from scratch is like assembling furniture from a flat pack — each piece (concept) was useless on its own, but together they create something functional. The grade calculator will accept student names and scores, compute averages, assign letter grades, and generate a report. You have already learned every piece you need — now you just arrange them.

sections.title: A helper function to calculate letter grade
sections.content: Converts a numeric score to a letter grade using if-else conditions

sections.title: Thinking in Steps: Planning Before Coding
sections.content: Before writing any code, break the problem into steps. First, create the data (student names and scores). Second, calculate each student's average. Third, convert averages to letter grades. Fourth, display a formatted report. This step-by-step approach — called decomposition — is how professional programmers tackle large problems. You are not writing one massive program; you are writing several small pieces that each do one thing well, then connecting them.

sections.title: Trying to write everything at once
sections.content: The #1 mistake beginners make on projects is writing all the code before testing any of it. Wrong: write 50 lines, run it, and nothing works — no idea which part broke. Correct: write the grade calculator function, test it. Write the average calculator function, test it. Build incrementally, testing each piece. Use console.log after every step to verify the data looks right.

sections.title: Your turn
sections.prompt: Build a grade report system. Create an array of student objects, each with a name and an array of scores. Write a function that calculates each student's average, assigns a letter grade using getLetterGrade, and prints a report like 'Alice: 85 (B)'. Test with three students: Alice [88, 92, 85], Bob [70, 65, 72], Charlie [95, 98, 100].
hints: What structure should each student object have — what properties?
How do you loop over the scores array to calculate a sum, then divide for the average?
Where does getLetterGrade need to be called — inside the loop or outside?
